en.summary: The Prophet Muhammad (SAWW) identified four emotional dispositions that form the foundations of disbelief (kufr): desire (raghba), which drives one to pursue worldly gains at the expense of faith; fear (rahba), which leads to cowardice in upholding truth; discontent (sakhat), which breeds ingratitude toward divine decree; and anger (ghadab), which clouds sound judgment. This teaching was delivered to the community as a warning about the psychological roots that undermine faith.

key_terms.أركان: Pillars, foundational supports — the essential components without which something cannot stand
key_terms.الكفر: Disbelief, rejection of faith — denial of God and His guidance
key_terms.الرغبة: Desire, worldly craving — excessive attachment to material gain
key_terms.الرهبة: Fear, dread — cowardly fear that prevents one from upholding truth
key_terms.السخط: Discontent, displeasure — being dissatisfied with divine decree
key_terms.الغضب: Anger, wrath — uncontrolled rage that overwhelms reason

en.seo_question: What are the four pillars of disbelief according to Prophet Muhammad (SAWW)?

ur.summary: رسول اللہ صلی اللہ علیہ وآلہ وسلم نے کفر کے چار ارکان بیان فرمائے: لالچ جو انسان کو دنیا کی طرف کھینچتی ہے، خوف جو حق کی حمایت سے روکتا ہے، ناراضگی جو تقدیر الہی پر اعتراض پیدا کرتی ہے، اور غصہ جو عقل و فہم پر پردہ ڈالتا ہے۔

key_terms.أركان: ارکان، بنیادی ستون — وہ لازمی اجزاء جن کے بغیر کوئی چیز قائم نہیں رہ سکتی
key_terms.الكفر: کفر — خدا اور اس کی ہدایت کا انکار

ur.seo_question: رسول اللہ صلی اللہ علیہ وآلہ وسلم کے مطابق کفر کے چار ارکان کیا ہیں؟

tr.summary: Hz. Peygamber (s.a.v.s.) küfrün dört temel direğini açıklamıştır: dünyevi hırs, hakkı savunmaktan alıkoyan korku, ilahi takdire karşı hoşnutsuzluk ve aklı örten öfke.
tr.seo_question: Hz. Peygamber'e göre küfrün dört rüknü nedir?

fa.summary: پیامبر اکرم (ص) چهار رکن کفر را بیان فرمودند: آز و طمع که انسان را به دنیاپرستی می‌کشاند، ترس که مانع دفاع از حق می‌شود، ناخشنودی از تقدیر الهی و خشم که عقل را از کار می‌اندازد.
fa.seo_question: طبق فرموده پیامبر (ص) چهار رکن کفر چیست؟

id.summary: Nabi Muhammad SAW menyebutkan empat pilar kekufuran: keinginan duniawi yang berlebihan, ketakutan yang menghalangi penegakan kebenaran, ketidakpuasan terhadap takdir ilahi, dan kemarahan yang mengaburkan akal sehat.
id.seo_question: Apa empat rukun kekufuran menurut Nabi Muhammad SAW?

bn.summary: নবী মুহাম্মাদ (সা.) কুফরের চারটি মূল স্তম্ভ বর্ণনা করেছেন: লোভ যা মানুষকে দুনিয়ার দিকে টানে, ভয় যা সত্যের পক্ষে দাঁড়াতে বাধা দেয়, তকদিরের প্রতি অসন্তোষ এবং ক্রোধ যা বিবেককে আচ্ছন্ন করে।
bn.seo_question: নবী মুহাম্মাদ (সা.) অনুসারে কুফরের চারটি স্তম্ভ কী?

es.summary: El Profeta Muhammad (PBDCE) identificó cuatro disposiciones emocionales que constituyen los fundamentos de la incredulidad: el deseo desmedido, el miedo cobarde, el descontento con el decreto divino y la ira descontrolada.
es.seo_question: ¿Cuáles son los cuatro pilares de la incredulidad según el Profeta Muhammad?

fr.summary: Le Prophète Muhammad (PSLF) a identifié quatre dispositions émotionnelles qui constituent les fondements de la mécréance : le désir immodéré, la crainte lâche, le mécontentement face au décret divin et la colère incontrôlée.
fr.seo_question: Quels sont les quatre piliers de la mécréance selon le Prophète Muhammad ?

de.summary: Der Prophet Muhammad (FSMIUSF) benannte vier emotionale Dispositionen als Grundpfeiler des Unglaubens: übermäßige Begierde, feige Furcht, Unzufriedenheit mit dem göttlichen Ratschluss und unkontrollierter Zorn.
de.seo_question: Was sind die vier Säulen des Unglaubens laut Prophet Muhammad?

ru.summary: Пророк Мухаммад (ДБАР) назвал четыре эмоциональных состояния, составляющих основу неверия: вожделение, ведущее к мирской привязанности; страх, мешающий отстаивать истину; недовольство божественным предопределением; и гнев, помрачающий разум.
ru.seo_question: Каковы четыре столпа неверия по словам Пророка Мухаммада?

zh.summary: 先知穆罕默德（愿主赐福他和他的家属）指出了构成不信的四个根基：贪欲使人追逐世俗而远离信仰；恐惧使人怯于坚持真理；不满使人对神圣旨意心生怨恨；愤怒则蒙蔽理智。
zh.seo_question: 根据先知穆罕默德，不信的四个柱石是什么？

isnad_matn.isnad_ar: حَدَّثَنَا أَبِي رَحِمَهُ اللَّهُ قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا عَلِيُّ بْنُ إِبْرَاهِيمَ عَنْ أَبِيهِ إِبْرَاهِيمَ بْنِ هَاشِمٍ عَنِ الْحُسَيْنِ بْنِ يَزِيدَ النَّوْفَلِيِّ عَنْ إِسْمَاعِيلَ بْنِ مُسْلِمٍ السَّكُونِيِّ عَنِ الصَّادِقِ جَعْفَرِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ أَبِيهِ عَنْ آبَائِهِ عَلَيْهِمُ السَّلَامُ عَنْ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَآلِهِ قَالَ
isnad_matn.matn_ar: أَرْكَانُ الْكُفْرِ أَرْبَعَةٌ الرَّغْبَةُ وَالرَّهْبَةُ وَالسَّخَطُ وَالْغَضَبُ

narrators.known_identity: Ali ibn al-Husayn ibn Musa ibn Babawayh al-Qummi (d. 329 AH), father of al-Shaykh al-Saduq
Ali ibn Ibrahim ibn Hashim al-Qummi (d. ~329 AH), prominent Shia hadith scholar and Quran commentator
Ibrahim ibn Hashim al-Qummi (d. ~early 4th century AH), a key transmitter in Shia hadith tradition
al-Husayn ibn Yazid al-Nawfali (d. ~after 220 AH), a narrator from the companions of Imam al-Ridha (AS)
Isma'il ibn Muslim al-Sukuni, a Sunni narrator who transmitted from Imam al-Sadiq (AS)
Imam Ja'far al-Sadiq (AS), the sixth Imam (d. 148 AH)

narrators.ambiguity_note: Refers to Imam Muhammad al-Baqir (AS), the fifth Imam, based on the preceding narrator Imam al-Sadiq (AS)
narrators.known_identity: Imam Muhammad al-Baqir (AS), the fifth Imam (d. 114 AH)

narrators.ambiguity_note: Collective reference to the Imams and ancestors in the chain from Imam al-Baqir back to Imam Ali (AS)
narrators.known_identity: The preceding Imams: Imam Zayn al-Abidin, Imam al-Husayn, and Imam Ali (peace be upon them all)

key_phrases.phrase_en: May God's blessings be upon him and his family

similar_content_hints.description: Hadith in al-Kafi about the branches and pillars of faith (iman) contrasting with the pillars of disbelief, describing the components that uphold belief such as patience, certainty, justice, and struggle.
Narrations from Imam Ali (AS) in Nahj al-Balagha about anger being destructive to faith and reason, and the virtue of controlling one's temper.
Hadiths about contentment (rida) with divine decree being a foundation of faith, contrasting with discontent (sakhat) as a foundation of disbelief.
